mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals accelerating labor market recovery, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment drop, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized expansion in both manufacturing and construction sectors, confirming broad-based economic momentum that lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing, or the combined indicator produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in real GDP coupled with rising retail sales demonstrates robust aggregate demand recovery, which expands corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in periods of accelerating GDP and retail sales are statistically indistinguishable from flat markets, or the signal exhibits a persistent negative directional bias over a 5-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.25 percent signals rising inflation expectations that compress equity valuations and increase discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn positive in > 60% of instances following the CPI threshold breach, or the signal shows no statistical edge after controlling for FEDFUNDS movements.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarter-over-quarter increase in industrial production reflects expanding capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which directly supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The indicator produces a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following INDPRO increases are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market tightening cycle, which accelerates wage-driven consumption and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict returns better than a random walk over a 3-year out-of-sample period, or the interaction term shows a statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with increasing retail sales indicate a durable consumer demand rebound, which lifts cyclicals and financials within the index and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined demand signal produces a negative directional bias in forward returns during the specified regime, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
